item.dc:creator: Dan De Luce
Rebecca Shabad
Ryan J. Reilly
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday named an ally with no background in intelligence to oversee the nation’s spy agencies, taking the helm as the U.S. remains at war with Iran after a fresh round of peace talks stalled.

media:content.media:text: Bill Pulte.
media:content.media:description: Director of the Federal Housing Finance Agency Bill Pulte walks outside the White House, Tuesday, Sept. 2, 2025, in Washington.

item.title: Housing official who targeted Trump’s enemies is named director of intelligence

item.dc:creator: Sahil Kapur
Ryan J. Reilly
Frank Thorp V
item.description: The Trump administration signaled it is backing off on creating a $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization” fund announced by the Justice Department that could send money to allies of President Donald Trump.

media:content.media:text: President Trump Meets With His Cabinet At The White House
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump listens to members of his Cabinet speak during a meeting in the Cabinet Room of the White House on May 27, 2026 in Washington, DC.

item.title: Trump admin appears to back off $1.8B ‘anti-weaponization’ fund after rare GOP backlash

item.description: Voters are gearing up for a key primaries with razor-tight races in six states, including Iowa where Democrats are hoping to win a Senate seat in the traditionally Republican state. Meanwhile in California, former reality star Spencer Pratt is gaining late momentum in his push to lead Los Angeles, the nation’s second-largest city. NBC’s Liz Kreutz reports for TODAY.

media:content.media:title: Voters Head to Polls in Key States, Setting the Stage for Midterms
media:content.media:description: Voters are gearing up for a key primaries with razor-tight races in six states, including Iowa where Democrats are hoping to win a Senate seat in the traditionally Republican state. Meanwhile in California, former reality star Spencer Pratt is gaining late momentum in his push to lead Los Angeles, the nation’s second-largest city. NBC’s Liz Kreutz reports for TODAY.

item.title: Voters Head to Polls in Key States, Setting the Stage for Midterms

item.description: Watch live coverage as Secretary of State Marco Rubio testifies before the House Appropriations Subcommittee on National Security, Department of State, and Related Programs.

media:content.media:title: Rubio testifies at House hearing
media:content.media:description: Watch live coverage as Secretary of State Marco Rubio testifies before the House Appropriations Subcommittee on National Security, Department of State, and Related Programs.

item.title: Rubio testifies at House hearing

item.description: More than a dozen people were killed and hundreds more inured after an overnight barrage of Russian missiles and drones targeted the Ukrainian capital. Russia had previously warned that it intended “systematic strikes” on targets in Kyiv linked to Ukraine’s military, urging foreigners to leave.

media:content.media:title: Russia targets Kyiv with massive overnight attack
media:content.media:description: More than a dozen people were killed and hundreds more inured after an overnight barrage of Russian missiles and drones targeted the Ukrainian capital. Russia had previously warned that it intended “systematic strikes” on targets in Kyiv linked to Ukraine’s military, urging foreigners to leave.

item.title: Russia targets Kyiv with massive overnight attack

item.description: Hundreds of people gathered in Nanyuki, Kenya, to protest a U.S.-proposed Ebola quarantine center for American citizens exposed to the virus. Kenya does not have any suspected Ebola cases, but neighboring Uganda has reported nine and closed its border with the Democratic Republic of Congo.

media:content.media:title: Protests erupt over U.S. Ebola quarantine center in Kenya
media:content.media:description: Hundreds of people gathered in Nanyuki, Kenya, to protest a U.S.-proposed Ebola quarantine center for American citizens exposed to the virus. Kenya does not have any suspected Ebola cases, but neighboring Uganda has reported nine and closed its border with the Democratic Republic of Congo.

item.title: Protests erupt over U.S. Ebola quarantine center in Kenya

item.dc:creator: Tim Rohan
item.description: Matt Freese once wrote a college research project on penalty kicks. Now he’s competing to be the starting goalie for the U.S. men’s national team at this summer’s World Cup.

media:content.media:text: United States v Paraguay - International Friendly
New York City FC v Nashville SC

item.title: Meet the Harvard grad who might lead the U.S. to World Cup glory

item.description: Watch live coverage as Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche testifies before a House Appropriations Subcommittee hearing.

media:content.media:title: Acting Attorney General Blanche testifies at House hearing
media:content.media:description: Watch live coverage as Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche testifies before a House Appropriations Subcommittee hearing.

item.title: Acting Attorney General Blanche testifies at House hearing

item.dc:creator: Nicole Acevedo
item.description: The mayor of Newark is doubling down on his efforts to shut down Delaney Hall, the New Jersey immigration detention facility that has been at the center of protests for nearly two weeks, drawing demonstrators over allegations of poor conditions at the facility and counterprotesters voicing support for ICE

media:content.media:text: Newark Mayor Ras Baraka speaks outside Delaney Hall detention center in Newark on June 2, 2026.
media:content.media:description: Newark Mayor Ras Baraka speaks outside Delaney Hall detention center in Newark, N.J., on Tuesday.

media:content.media:text: Detainees stand by a window inside the federal immigration center.
media:content.media:description: Newark Mayor Ras Baraka speaks outside Delaney Hall detention center in Newark, N.J., on Tuesday.

media:content.media:text: Immigration Advocates Demonstrate And Clash With Officers Outside Delaney Hall Detention Facility In Newark
media:content.media:description: Newark Mayor Ras Baraka speaks outside Delaney Hall detention center in Newark, N.J., on Tuesday.

media:content.media:text: Immigration Protests New Jersey
media:content.media:description: Newark Mayor Ras Baraka speaks outside Delaney Hall detention center in Newark, N.J., on Tuesday.

media:content.media:text: APTOPIX Immigration Protests New Jersey
media:content.media:description: Newark Mayor Ras Baraka speaks outside Delaney Hall detention center in Newark, N.J., on Tuesday.

item.title: N.J. targets prison company running immigration detention center in lawsuits over conditions

item.dc:creator: Tim Rohan
item.description: Caught up in the NBA playoffs?

media:content.media:text: Taylor Hall skates in the hockey rink in uniform while holding a hockey stick
media:content.media:description: Taylor Hall of the Carolina Hurricanes warms up prior to Game Three of the Eastern Conference Final of the 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs against the Montréal Canadiens on May 25.

media:content.media:text: Mitch Marner, center, skates on the ice in a hockey uniform between other players
media:content.media:description: Taylor Hall of the Carolina Hurricanes warms up prior to Game Three of the Eastern Conference Final of the 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs against the Montréal Canadiens on May 25.

media:content.media:text: Jamal Musiala, right, jumps high while aiming to kick a ball on the soccer field next to other players
media:content.media:description: Taylor Hall of the Carolina Hurricanes warms up prior to Game Three of the Eastern Conference Final of the 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs against the Montréal Canadiens on May 25.

item.title: From The Sports Desk: Heavyweights Carolina, Vegas meet in Stanley Cup Final

item.dc:creator: Megan Lebowitz
item.description: President Donald Trump announced on Tuesday that he would attend the rescheduled White House Correspondents’ Association dinner in July after the spring event was abruptly cut short when a gunman ran through a security checkpoint.

media:content.media:text: President Trump Attends White House Correspondents' Association Dinner
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump during the White House Correspondents' Association dinner on April 25.

media:content.media:text: Trump
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump during the White House Correspondents' Association dinner on April 25.

item.title: Trump to speak at the rescheduled White House Correspondents’ Dinner

item.dc:creator: David K. Li
item.description: Mystery men who are “up to no good” have been roaming through New York City’s vast sewer system, and city officials on Tuesday warned potential copycats of dangers they’d face exploring Gotham’s treacherous underground

media:content.media:text: New video shows a group wearing hip waiters and carrying flash lights as they pry open a manhole cover on May 5th just before 2 a.m. in Astoria.
media:content.media:description: New video shows a group prying open a manhole cover on May 5, 2026, just before 2 a.m. in Astoria, Queens.

item.title: NYC officials warn of ‘extremely dangerous’ sewers after mystery men emerge from manholes

item.dc:creator: Jared Perlo
item.description: President Trump signed an order seeking the establishment of a voluntary framework for AI companies to provide the government early access to new models.

media:content.media:text: Trump
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump signs an executive order in the Oval Office of the White House on April 30.

item.title: Trump signs order seeking early access to powerful AI models before release

item.dc:creator: Dan De Luce
Rebecca Shabad
Ryan J. Reilly
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday named an ally with no background in intelligence to oversee the nation’s spy agencies, taking the helm as the U.S. remains at war with Iran after a fresh round of peace talks stalled.

media:content.media:text: Bill Pulte.
media:content.media:description: Director of the Federal Housing Finance Agency Bill Pulte walks outside the White House, Tuesday, Sept. 2, 2025, in Washington.

item.title: Housing official who targeted Trump’s enemies is named director of intelligence

item.dc:creator: NBC News
item.description: NBC News’ chief international correspondent will be in St. Petersburg, Russia, for a live Q&A. Submit your questions here.

media:content.media:text: Keir Simmons on "Meet the Press."
media:content.media:description: Keir Simmons.

item.title: Submit your questions for NBC News’ Keir Simmons

item.dc:creator: Mithil Aggarwal
item.description: Plans for Americans exposed to Ebola to be quarantined abroad faced mounting backlash Tuesday, with Kenya’s president defending a proposed 50-bed facility in the country after violent protests

media:content.media:text: APTOPIX Kenya Ebola
media:content.media:description: A protester raises a Kenyan flag near a burning barricade during a demonstration against a proposed Ebola quarantine center to be established by the United States at Laikipia Air Base in Nanyuki, Kenya, Monday, June 1, 2026.

media:content.media:text: Protesters demonstrate the Ebola treatment center established or Americans in Kenya
media:content.media:description: Protesters march toward the entrance of Laikipia air base in Nanyuki, Kenya, on Monday.

media:content.media:text: Protesters demonstrate the Ebola treatment center established or Americans in Kenya
media:content.media:description: Protesters near a fire near the Laikipia air base.

item.title: U.S. plan to open Ebola quarantine center in Kenya faces growing backlash and protests

item.dc:creator: Chantal Da Silva
item.description: The Israeli military launched deadly new strikes in Lebanon on Tuesday after accusing Hezbollah of launching attacks overnight, despite President Donald Trump saying both sides had agreed to de-escalate after Iran threatened to pull out of peace talks

media:content.media:text: Smoke rises from an Israeli airstrike that hit Qlaileh village, as seen from the southern port city of Tyre, Lebanon,
media:content.media:description: Smoke rises from an Israeli airstrike that hit Qlaileh village, as seen from the southern port city of Tyre, Lebanon, Tuesday, June 2, 2026.

media:content.media:text: TOPSHOT-LEBANON-ISRAEL-IRAN-US-WAR
media:content.media:description: A hospital damaged by an Israeli strike that hit nearby, in the southern Lebanese city of Tyre on Monday.

media:content.media:text: Lebanon Israel Iran War
media:content.media:description: Rescuers work at the site of an Israeli airstrike that hit a building and damaged a hospital in the southern port city of Tyre, Lebanon, on Monday.

item.title: Israel and Hezbollah trade new attacks despite Trump promise of de-escalation

item.dc:creator: Tim Rohan
item.description: Matt Freese once wrote a college research project on penalty kicks. Now he’s competing to be the starting goalie for the U.S. men’s national team at this summer’s World Cup.

media:content.media:text: United States v Paraguay - International Friendly
New York City FC v Nashville SC

item.title: Meet the Harvard grad who might lead the U.S. to World Cup glory

item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
Madison Lambert
item.description: Authorities in Iowa are investigating the fatal shootings of six people who they believe were killed by a relative who took his own life when confronted by police Monday

media:content.media:text: The Muscatine Chief of Police said six people were found dead Monday afternoon and that the suspected gunman fatally shot himself.
media:content.media:description: Police were called Monday to a home in Muscatine, Iowa, where they found four people fatally shot.

item.title: Iowa gunman suspected of killing 6 family members then himself

item.dc:creator: Ben Kamisar
Bridget Bowman
Adam Edelman
Alexandra Marquez
item.description: The fight to shape the future of the Democratic Party is playing out in a slew of key primaries Tuesday, as races in six states set the matchups in crucial November battlegrounds and settle personal and ideological battles across the country

media:content.media:text: A still taken from California governor's debate.
media:content.media:description: A still taken from California governor's debate.

item.title: Democratic battles and incumbents on defense: What to watch in Tuesday’s primaries

item.dc:creator: Cory Fernandez
Cory Fernandez
Bethany Heitman
item.description: I found the best methods and products for cleaning glasses, including reading glasses, sunglasses and more. Optometrists recommend the best cleaning products.

media:content.media:text: A microfiber cloth is one of the best options for cleaning glasses since it won’t leave streaks.
media:content.media:description: A microfiber cloth is one of the best options for cleaning glasses since it won’t leave streaks.

media:content.media:text: A microfiber cloth is one of the best options for cleaning glasses since it won’t leave streaks.
media:content.media:description: A microfiber cloth is one of the best options for cleaning glasses since it won’t leave streaks.

item.title: This is how you should actually be cleaning your glasses

item.dc:creator: Bethany Heitman
item.description: Sandals are notoriously not great for lots of walking. These podiatrists-approved sandals have built-in arch support and cushioning to keep you comfortable.

media:content.media:text: According to podiatrists, the key to a comfy sandal is built-in arch support and thick, secure straps.
media:content.media:description: According to podiatrists, the key to a comfy sandal is built-in arch support and thick, secure straps.

media:content.media:text: According to podiatrists, the key to a comfy sandal is built-in arch support and thick, secure straps.
media:content.media:description: According to podiatrists, the key to a comfy sandal is built-in arch support and thick, secure straps.

item.title: 6 podiatrist-approved sandals to comfortably walk in all summer

item.dc:creator: Bianca Alvarez
item.description: Bar soaps work as effectively as body washes and I rounded up editor-approved picks from Dove, L’Occitane, Avène and more.

media:content.media:text: Yes, they clean your skin, but they can also combat acne, dryness and uneven texture.
media:content.media:description: Yes, they clean your skin, but they can also combat acne, dryness and uneven texture.

media:content.media:text: Yes, they clean your skin, but they can also combat acne, dryness and uneven texture.
media:content.media:description: Yes, they clean your skin, but they can also combat acne, dryness and uneven texture.

item.title: Bar soaps vs. body wash — which is best for your skin?

item.dc:creator: Bethany Heitman
item.description: Podiatrists share their product recommendations and shopping tips for supportive, comfortable insoles that can help with arch pain, plantar fasciitis and more.

media:content.media:text: According to the podiatrists we spoke to, over-the-counter insoles work best in sneakers.
media:content.media:description: According to the podiatrists we spoke to, over-the-counter insoles work best in sneakers.

media:content.media:text: According to the podiatrists we spoke to, over-the-counter insoles work best in sneakers.
media:content.media:description: According to the podiatrists we spoke to, over-the-counter insoles work best in sneakers.

item.title: The best insoles for your sneakers

item.dc:creator: Evan Bush
item.description: Hurricane season in the Atlantic kicked off Monday, and for the first time in more than a decade, forecasters expect it to be slower than usual.

media:content.media:text: Hurricane Melissa makes landfall as Jamaica's strongest-ever storm
media:content.media:description: A satellite view of Hurricane Melissa over the Caribbean Sea, near New Hope, Jamaica, in October.

media:content.media:text: Hawaii Floods
media:content.media:description: A satellite view of Hurricane Melissa over the Caribbean Sea, near New Hope, Jamaica, in October.

item.title: Hurricane season begins, with the first below-average forecast in a decade
